programme.title: The Blue Realm Ep114: Shark Divers Part 2
programme.desc: Shark divers study, photograph and interact with some of the most terrifying creatures on earth. This group of skilled professionals routinely work with sharks out in the wild and in aquariums. Shark Divers focuses on this diverse and eclectic dive group…each with different motives in their fascinating fields.

programme.title: Airtime Ep41: Kayaking The Mighty Rivers of Africa
programme.desc: The world’s top kayakers travel Africa in search of the most dangerous whitewater. Shot over the course of a year, this episode highlights the travel and adventure of world-class kayakers as they embark on a “bigger and better” hunt for the largest waterfalls in a land where emergency services are virtually non-existent.

programme.title: Blue World Ep612: Global Search for the Rare Dugongs + Cave Diving in Florida, USA
programme.desc: Blue World travels the world on a hunt for Dugongs… rare, shy animals resembling manatees that are very difficult to film. But we get lucky in the Philippines with an incredible once-in-a-lifetime encounter! Then, an amazing cave dive at Peacock Springs in Florida, where Blue World divers do their first “traverse”.

programme.title: World of Watersports Ep306: Team Sailing Championships + Kitefoil Grand Prix + European Sailing Championships - Austria
programme.desc: This episode of World of Watersports highlights the Red Bull Sailing Team on Lake Constance near the German/Austria border, the Kitefoil Grand Prix in Traunsee, Austria, and the European Sailing Championships in Attersee, Austria.

programme.title: Farm Hero
programme.desc: In the season premiere of Farm Hero, host Laura Zaspel scours the country for the best farming practices and the people behind them. Her search winds her up in Gunnison Colorado at Parker pastures. Bill Parker and his daughters are fighting desertification with their grass-fed livestock. Using a collaboration with local farmers, Bill fights against the stigma of livestock farming with a holistic approach. Parker pastures breathes new air into the concept of farm-to-table.

programme.title: Farm Hero
programme.desc: Ever-increasing rainfall, coupled with monocultural farming, is eradicating the topsoil in Iowa farms. With an estimate that all topsoil in the area will be gone within 35 years, the race is on to change farming culture. Elizabeth Garst and her farm are at the forefront of the fight to educate farmers and the public on the necessity of cover crops and soil health. Elizabeth’s ancestors turned Dust Bowl land into a thriving farm with morals, and she’s not stopping there.

programme.title: Boom America
programme.desc: Boom America is kicking off with a bang, led by the legendary shark himself, Kevin Harrington. Backed by his team of successful entrepreneurs, Kevin meets with businesses to jumpstart their success and take them to a whole new level. From Nuethix, a supplement line struggling with client acquisition and education, to Stream2Sea, a eco-friendly sunscreen wanting to become a household name—Kevin and the A-team are ready to jump in and get their hands dirty.

programme.title: Boom America
programme.desc: It’s time to go to work. Now that we’ve met our companies, Kevin and the A-team are ready to hit the ground running. NuEhtix, Dr Price Vitamins, Stream2Sea and Clear Water get a deep dive round table session from Kevin and His A-team. We also get to meet the A-Team players and what make them titans in their trades. Tune in to see the insights the team come up with and how the companies apply it to build their brands.

Follow them Beyond The Close.

programme.title: Inside Pro Surfing: Rip Curl Pro Bells Beach presented by Bonsoy 2025
programme.desc: Bells Beach on Victoria's South Coast is the longest-running surfing competition and will host the 62st edition this year. Bells is Australian surfing heritage. With the hope of scoring long-period, groomed walls, the surfers will need to display the perfect combination of power, precision, and timing. The biggest surfing legends have had the honor of winning and ringing ‘The Bell' and 2025 will see a hungry new generation vying for the opportunity to cement their names in one of surfing's most prestigious events.

programme.title: African Renaissance | Brilliant Corners: Senegal, Episode 3
programme.desc: Sam, Easkey, and Adrien catch up with West African surf legend Oumar Seye, the first West African sponsored surfer. Seye has impacted the surf culture in Senegal in more ways than one, both proving the sport can be more than a hobby and supporting sustainability by banning plastic bags at his surf events. They end their trip in Popenguine, a bohemian beach town where an incredible women-led initiative to plant thousands of trees has made the area one of the greenest parts of the country.
